round1.whitelistVersion: sha256:pre-cid-widening (pre-existing tree state, no recorded hash)
round1.rootCause: MHTML body-side cid:<UUID>@mhtml.blink references + ------MultipartBoundary--…---- separators not whitelisted (only the Content-ID: header line and Content-Type header boundary= param were). Same RFC 2557 per-snapshot-random mechanism as the already-whitelisted header. Widened in commit: HTML_ATTR_WHITELIST_PATTERNS now masks cid:-refs and inline boundary=, MHTML_WHITELIST_LINE_PATTERNS now drops body separator lines; normalize step now joins QP soft-line-breaks first so wrapped UUIDs match.

round2_reEval_without_refreeze.whitelistVersion: sha256:pre-Laboratory-row (pre-existing tree state, no recorded hash)
round2_reEval_without_refreeze.rootCause: Residual drift dominated by HubSpot Laboratory experiment ID — <meta name="laboratory-identifier-other" content="anon<32hex>"> rotates per session (L212), driving body-structure variance at L213 (one freeze starts <body> with <div id=hs-web-interactives-…>; another inserts a hidden bot-tarpit <a tabindex=-1 aria-hidden=true>). The line-shift cascade after L213 explains the inflated drift count — most lines past L213 differ only by index shift, not content. The hint regex correctly flagged 'unclassified' because Laboratory was not in MECHANISM_HINTS.

whitelistRowsChanged: Laboratory row narrowed: envelope is now <meta name="laboratory-identifier-*" content="anon<32hex>"> attribute value ONLY. Body-structure clause removed.

scripts/freeze-determinism-check.ts: HTML_ATTR_WHITELIST_PATTERNS gained a laboratory-identifier meta-attr mask; MECHANISM_HINTS gained ab:hubspot-laboratory so future runs classify L212/L213 explicitly.
round3_post_narrowing.evaluationMode: logical re-evaluation against round2's recorded line-level diff (only run-0 MHTML is stashed; round3 cannot do a full pairwise re-freeze without a fresh N=3 Browserbase capture).
round3_post_narrowing.rootCause: Laboratory-meta-attr drift at L212 is now masked → does not contribute. Body-structure drift at L213 (presence/absence of <a tabindex=-1 aria-hidden=true opacity:0.01> bot-tarpit anchor as direct child of <body>) is NOT whitelisted by the narrowed row and is NOT inside any identified personalization slot, so it surfaces as RED per the 'What is NOT whitelisted' rule. The L213+ line-shift cascade remains. Hint output for L213 will classify as 'unclassified' (no MECHANISM_HINTS entry for bot-tarpit injection — by design; injection is heuristic/bot-score-driven, not a documented mechanism).
round3_post_narrowing.consequence: Hubspot fails capture-determinism (promotion criterion #3 in corpus/README.md). corpus/hubspot/ MUST be flagged pending-determinism. Re-promotion requires either (a) a fresh N=3 freeze where the bot-tarpit anchor is absent across all runs, or (b) a separate workstream that measures whether the score consumes body-structure variance — if score is invariant under the tarpit-anchor toggle, the variance is sample-defining-but-score-neutral and can be re-whitelisted with explicit envelope + evidence.

verdict: RED (read evidence-driven; body-structure drift remains unwhitelisted post-narrowing)
interpretation: Hubspot has TWO distinct sources of session-variance: (1) HubSpot Laboratory experiment ID — a-priori benign session token, now narrowly whitelisted by attribute-value envelope; (2) bot-tarpit anchor injection — heuristic/bot-score-driven, NOT bucket-deterministic for the same client, categorically distinct from Optimizely-style A/B and NOT a personalization slot. Source (2) keeps hubspot in RED until either source (2) stops varying across captures or score-impact is measured to be zero.
implications: MHTML transport-layer whitelist widened in scripts/freeze-determinism-check.ts and locked in WHITELIST.md (rounds 1-2).
HubSpot Laboratory row narrowed in WHITELIST.md (round 3) — attribute-value envelope only, body-structure clause removed.
corpus/hubspot/ flagged pending-determinism in meta.json with pointer to this diff as evidence.
Score-impact measurement of bot-tarpit anchor on the 3 stashed MHTMLs remains a separate workstream — prerequisite for any re-promotion of hubspot to a clean golden-bearing corpus member.
stashed: fixtures/determinism/hubspot/run-0.mhtml (one of the 3 freezes preserved for offline re-evaluation)
